title: Astraea surinamensis
intro: Astraea surinamensis, also known as the Surinam queen's crown or Queen's Crown, is a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. The plant is valued for its traditional medicinal uses, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Astraea surinamensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used in traditional medicine to promote healing and well-being.
